2605.12438.title: A Causal Language Modeling Detour Improves Encoder Continued Pretraining
2605.12438.paper_summary: When adapting an encoder to a new domain, the standard approach is to continue training with Masked Language Modeling (MLM). We show that temporarily switching to Causal Language Modeling (CLM) followed by a short MLM decay improves downstream performance. On biomedical texts with ModernBERT, this CLM detour outperforms MLM baselines trained on identical data and compute across 8 French and 11 English biomedical tasks, by +1.2-2.8pp and +0.3-0.8pp respectively, depending on model size. We investigate the reasons for these gains. We find that CLM's dense supervision impacts low transformer layers (0-7) far more than MLM does. Freezing low layers during CLM eliminates the downstream benefit; freezing mid layers preserves it. The representational changes persist through the MLM decay phase, even when it matches the CLM phase in length, and they scale with model capacity. We release ModernCamemBERT-bio and ModernBERT-bio as state-of-the-art biomedical encoders in Base and Large sizes.
2605.12438.paper_summary_zh: 當將編碼器適應於新領域時，標準方法是繼續使用遮蔽語言建模（MLM）進行訓練。我們顯示，暫時切換到因果語言建模（CLM），隨後進行短期的MLM衰減，可以改善下游性能。在使用ModernBERT的生物醫學文本中，這種CLM繞道在8個法語和11個英語生物醫學任務中，超越了在相同數據和計算上訓練的MLM基準，分別提高了+1.2-2.8個百分點和+0.3-0.8個百分點，具體取決於模型大小。我們調查了這些增益的原因。我們發現，CLM的密集監督對低層Transformer層（0-7）的影響遠大於MLM。在CLM期間凍結低層會消除下游的好處；凍結中層則能保留這一好處。這些表示變化在MLM衰減階段持續存在，即使其長度與CLM階段相匹配，並且它們隨模型容量而增長。我們發布了ModernCamemBERT-bio和ModernBERT-bio，作為基礎和大型尺寸的最先進生物醫學編碼器。
2605.12438.author: Rian Touchent et.al.
2605.12438.authors: Rian Touchent, Eric de la Clergerie
